title: fix(klein): return 400 for invalid reference image input
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Bad reference-image uploads (malformed base64 / non-image bytes) raised an unhandled exception in the klein handler and surfaced as **500s**.
- Logs show these are ~97% of klein's 5xx (`PIL.UnidentifiedImageError`) — all user bad-input, not infra failures.
- Wrap the decode/open step and raise `HTTPException(400)` instead, so genuine 5xx (e.g. CUDA OOM) stay visible in the error rate.
- Catches `UnidentifiedImageError`, `binascii.Error`, and `ValueError`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Polite rejections for bad pixels
gist.blurb: We've updated the image generation API to return a helpful 400 Bad Request instead of a 500 error when you accidentally send us malformed reference images. Easier debugging for you, cleaner logs for us!
gist.summary: The Klein image handler now returns a 400 Bad Request instead of a 500 Internal Server Error when receiving malformed base64 or non-image bytes for reference images.
gist.impact: Developers sending invalid reference images will now receive a clear 400 error, making it easier to debug client-side issues while keeping our 500 error metrics accurate for real infrastructure problems.
gist.visual_concept: The bee (Polly) and Nomnom creature. Nomnom is trying to eat a corrupted/glitchy image file and spitting it out, while Polly holds up a polite '400' sign instead of a scary '500' sign.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot holding up a polite '400' sign while the round tan Nomnom creature spits out a glitchy, corrupted image file. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
